blockingIssues.description: The mission validation contract requires relevant network-call evidence for VAL-LIFE-001, VAL-LIFE-002, VAL-LIFE-003, and VAL-LIFE-005, but `/Users/aischool/.factory/missions/c506faaa-7d1c-4db2-be71-183035095277/evidence/raw/lifecycle-browser-network.txt` contains only `No requests captured`, leaving those lifecycle assertions incompletely evidenced.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update scrutiny-feature-reviewer or scrutiny-validator guidance to explicitly handle mission-directory artifacts whose real deliverables live outside the repo working tree, because a handoff commitId may not expose the feature output.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The failed review for `lifecycle-browser-api-audit` noted that handoff commit `50fe2196f9058765cdff0a788f876e3f312e7e13` only showed unrelated repo changes while the feature's real deliverable was `/Users/aischool/.factory/missions/c506faaa-7d1c-4db2-be71-183035095277/evidence/lifecycle.md` and its raw mission evidence.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify whether `audit-worker` must always read `package.json`, or relax that step when equivalent execution metadata already comes from `.factory/services.yaml` and repository docs, so reviews can judge compliance consistently.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `lifecycle-browser-api-audit` review observed that `audit-worker` marked `followedProcedure: true` even though the transcript skeleton did not include the package.json read currently described in the skill instructions.
